action.reasoning: Observation: val_eval on ShowUI val split (same domain, image-disjoint) gives 0.0 grounding_score (mean_iou=0.0199); model outputs valid <box> format but coords off (e.g. y=145 vs GT~39 on 1000-grid for wifi toggle, IoU<<0.5). Full test gets 0.0006 (1/1581). Training reaches low loss (~0.28) showing format/coord regression learned on train but poor generalization due to low-res training (256*28*28 loses UI details), domain gap to ScreenSpot-Pro, and limited epochs. Token/feature match fixed; merged full-model save works for eval; parsers/rescale/clip/defaults/JSON solid and prompt-matched. Analysis: Low-res during SFT primary culprit for bad localization on complex pro-UI screenshots; higher res (512*28*28) + LoRA r=32 + slightly higher LR + metrics.jsonl + full non-truncated code + merge_at_end will improve detail capture and coord accuracy. With ~3h/2 iters left, one final ~10k-step train (~2.5h) on full data should push test grounding_score >0.05-0.15 before final submit. Decision: Overwrite train.py (compact complete, higher res=512*28*28, r=32, lr=5e-5, 10000 steps, proper jsonl logging every 100 steps, merge+save full model/processor/grid) and predict.py (match max_pixels=512*28*28, same prompt, max_new_tokens=128); run train then submit_eval on resulting checkpoint.
